one_liner: PLTR at $129 is consolidating in its Wyckoff range (support $114, resistance $153) with 37% DCF upside and positive MACD — but a research firm published a bearish 'trap' note calling the 23% bounce off lows a false dawn.

price_action.vol_rank: Below average — moderate volume on -2.41% day
price_action.intraday_story: PLTR dipped to $124.81 intraday, recovered to close at $129.04. The close was above the open — a bullish recapture after testing intraday lows. Volume was below average, suggesting the dip was not driven by aggressive selling.

news_catalyst.headline: Research Firm Warns 23% Palantir Bounce is a 'Trap' — Won't Rate It a Buy
news_catalyst.summary: A research firm published a negative note calling the recent 23% Palantir recovery from lows a 'trap' rather than a genuine reversal. Despite this, the stock held above EMA200 ($129.28) and EMA50 ($127.39). The divergence between technical resilience and analyst skepticism creates a watch — not a clear accumulate.

technical.summary: PLTR is holding between EMA50 ($127.39) and EMA200 ($129.28) — a very tight band. The stock closed at $129.04, essentially at the EMA200. RSI 53.6 and MACD histogram +1.631 are both mildly positive. Wyckoff range support at $114.45, resistance at $152.93. The 37% DCF upside is compelling but the research firm 'trap' warning and the elevated valuation concerns argue for watching until a clear directional signal emerges.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: Holding at EMA cluster. Need a close above EMA200 ($129.28) + upper BB ($142) for an accumulate signal. August 3 earnings will be the next major catalyst.

valuation.summary: DCF $177 vs $129 — 37% upside is meaningful. Grade B fundamentals support but research bearishness creates uncertainty.

key_strengths: Government AI contracts provide revenue visibility
key_concerns: Research firm bearish note this week
Premium valuation relative to current revenue scale
fundamentals.summary: B grade fundamentals with strong government contract pipeline. The research firm 'trap' warning creates near-term caution.

risks: Close below EMA50 at $127.39 would confirm the research firm's 'trap' thesis
August 3 earnings — any guidance miss on commercial AIP growth would trigger selling
